metadata.title: Product Experiences
metadata.description: Outcome-based product experiences for the Municipal Public Space Enforcement domain, centered on municipal nuisance reduction, enforceable case flow, coordinated field action, and compliant public-space operations.

definitions.productBrick: A reusable implementation-facing building block used to realize one or more product experiences.
definitions.productCapability: A high-level, outcome-based experience that delivers customer or business value.

metadata.flowModelNote: Each experience carries only experience-specific flows. Each flow step lists the bricks and external systems used inside that experience.

capabilities.name: Detect and Qualify Illegal Dumping
capabilities.description: Help councils identify likely dumping incidents quickly and focus operator time on the hotspots and events most likely to matter.

flows.title: Detect and Qualify Illegal Dumping
flows.description: Move from hotspot planning to a qualified incident ready for action.

steps.title: Select high-value hotspot
keyFacts: Choosing the right first sites strongly shapes visible municipal outcomes.
Councils need confidence that the deployment will target a real problem, not just add hardware.
painPoints: Poor siting decisions waste budget and weaken political confidence.
Teams lose trust when coverage stays active on low-value sites while major hotspots persist.

dependencies.name: Deployment Planning and Site Selection
dependencies.description: Prioritizes which public-space hotspots should be instrumented, how long equipment should remain in place, and when a site should be moved or escalated.
dependencies.how: Uses Deployment Planning and Site Selection to prioritize which hotspot should be instrumented first.

dependencies.name: Geospatial Incident Map
dependencies.description: Maps incidents, tasks, recurrence, and deployment footprint across the municipal territory.
dependencies.how: Uses Geospatial Incident Map to understand recurrence and territorial context before deployment.

steps.title: Capture suspected incident
keyFacts: The field network must work in low-infrastructure conditions and remain proportionate.
Capture quality is useless if connectivity or privacy posture fails.
painPoints: Deployments fail when devices need heavy civil works or unstable local infrastructure.
Capture becomes unusable when privacy boundaries are unclear or inconsistently applied.

dependencies.name: Edge Camera and Sensor Unit
dependencies.description: The movable field hardware surface that captures suspicious activity and media evidence in selected public-space zones.
dependencies.how: Uses Edge Camera and Sensor Unit to capture event-triggered media on the hotspot.

dependencies.name: Secure 4G/5G Connectivity Gateway
dependencies.description: Provides secure transport between field devices and the EU control plane without relying on municipal fiber rollout.
dependencies.how: Uses Secure 4G/5G Connectivity Gateway to transport field events into the live control plane.

dependencies.name: Privacy Zoning and Masking
dependencies.description: Applies configured capture boundaries and masking rules so monitoring remains focused on the relevant public area.
dependencies.how: Uses Privacy Zoning and Masking to keep capture within the approved public-space zone.

steps.title: Qualify real incident
keyFacts: Operators only trust the flow when likely actionability is clear before case work starts.
Spatial and historical context help distinguish routine noise from hotspot patterns.
painPoints: False positives waste officer and cleanup capacity.
Weak prioritization leads to delayed action on the incidents that matter most.

dependencies.name: Incident Detection Rules Engine
dependencies.description: Evaluates field signals and media events to identify suspicious activity likely to represent a municipal offence or nuisance incident.
dependencies.how: Uses Incident Detection Rules Engine to create candidate incidents from field events.

dependencies.name: AI-assisted Incident Triage
dependencies.description: Helps operators review, prioritize, and qualify likely actionable incidents before they become cases or field tasks.
dependencies.how: Uses AI-assisted Incident Triage to rank the incidents most likely to become meaningful municipal action.

capabilities.name: Convert Incidents into Enforceable Cases
capabilities.description: Help operators secure admissible evidence, follow the right procedure, and issue a formal sanction or partner handoff.

flows.title: Convert Incidents into Enforceable Cases
flows.description: Move from a qualified incident to a procedurally ready enforcement file.

steps.title: Secure evidence
keyFacts: Evidence must remain admissible and accountable from the beginning of case work.
Retention and access state are as important as the media itself.
painPoints: Cases break when evidence provenance is weak or access is uncontrolled.
Manual evidence handling creates hidden compliance risk.

dependencies.name: Evidence Vault and Chain of Custody
dependencies.description: Stores evidence, metadata, timestamps, and provenance needed to support enforceable municipal procedures.
dependencies.how: Uses Evidence Vault and Chain of Custody to preserve the case evidence set.

dependencies.name: Access Control and Audit Trail
dependencies.description: Controls who can access cases and evidence, and provides full traceability over key actions across the platform.
dependencies.how: Uses Access Control and Audit Trail to maintain traceable evidence access.

steps.title: Build case record
keyFacts: Operators need one coherent place to progress a case and understand its state.
Workflow clarity matters more than raw document volume.
painPoints: Case work slows when facts are scattered across separate tools and folders.
Teams miss steps when the workflow does not clearly expose what comes next.

dependencies.name: Enforcement Case Workspace
dependencies.description: Organizes incidents, evidence, people, case state, and actions into one municipal case record.
dependencies.how: Uses Enforcement Case Workspace to assemble and progress the municipal case.

dependencies.name: Legal Procedure Templates
dependencies.description: Encodes reusable municipal procedure paths, checklists, and required documents for enforcement cases.
dependencies.how: Uses Legal Procedure Templates to guide what the case needs next.

steps.title: Issue notice or transfer
keyFacts: The value is only realized when the procedural pack is usable by the next authority.
Finance and partner handoff should not require manual repackaging.
painPoints: Sanctions stall when notices are incomplete or misaligned with the case record.
Partner transfers fail when shared material is inconsistent or weakly governed.

dependencies.name: Notice and Fine Pack Generator
dependencies.description: Generates the notice, sanction, and supporting procedural material required to move a case into formal municipal action.
dependencies.how: Uses Notice and Fine Pack Generator to produce the formal sanction package.

dependencies.name: Fine Recovery and Treasury Integration
dependencies.description: Tracks sanctions and payment state and connects enforcement outcomes to treasury or municipal finance workflows.
dependencies.how: Uses Fine Recovery and Treasury Integration to connect the case to recovery workflow.

dependencies.name: Interagency Evidence Sharing
dependencies.description: Packages and transmits controlled case and evidence material to partner authorities or municipal services.
dependencies.how: Uses Interagency Evidence Sharing when the case must be transferred to a partner authority.

capabilities.name: Coordinate Field Response and Cleanup
capabilities.description: Help municipal police, technical services, and crews act quickly on validated incidents and reduce recurrence on treated hotspots.
outcomes: Lower recurrence and fewer repeat visits

flows.title: Coordinate Field Response and Cleanup
flows.description: Move from validated incident to field action and recurrence-aware follow-up.

steps.title: Create service task
keyFacts: Field actors need enough context before they travel.
Shared task state avoids email and phone reconciliation.
painPoints: Low-context dispatch creates repeat visits and slow closure.
Disconnected tasking makes it hard to understand what happened on site.

dependencies.name: Field Service Task Dispatch
dependencies.description: Assigns operational work to municipal services, cleanup crews, and field actors with enough context to act once.
dependencies.how: Uses Field Service Task Dispatch to route the validated incident to the right municipal actor.

steps.title: Complete field action
keyFacts: Field actors must be able to confirm what happened without returning to the office first.
Mobile closure quality shapes the value of downstream analytics.
painPoints: Teams lose evidence of operational action when mobile updates are weak or delayed.
Managers cannot trust closure metrics when field status is inconsistent.

dependencies.name: Officer Mobile Workflow
dependencies.description: Supports field validation, intervention notes, task completion, and case follow-up from mobile surfaces.
dependencies.how: Uses Officer Mobile Workflow to capture what happened in the field.

steps.title: Review recurrence pattern
keyFacts: Intervention only creates durable value when the municipality learns whether the hotspot improved.
Spatial and longitudinal views are needed to decide whether to keep, move, or escalate.
painPoints: Councils keep spending on cleanup when recurrence insight is weak.
Teams relocate devices on intuition instead of evidence.

dependencies.name: Geospatial Incident Map
dependencies.description: Maps incidents, tasks, recurrence, and deployment footprint across the municipal territory.
dependencies.how: Uses Geospatial Incident Map to compare hotspot state before and after field action.

dependencies.name: Outcome Analytics and Benchmarking
dependencies.description: Computes outcome metrics, recurrence trends, benchmark views, and longitudinal value proof across sites and municipalities.
dependencies.how: Uses Outcome Analytics and Benchmarking to quantify recurrence and intervention performance.

capabilities.name: Show Residents Visible Results
capabilities.description: Help councils demonstrate that public-space action is real, measurable, and proportionate.

flows.title: Show Residents Visible Results
flows.description: Turn operational data into public and executive proof that municipal action is working.

steps.title: Assemble outcome proof
keyFacts: Leaders need clean outcome metrics, not raw incident volume.
Municipal value proof needs both service and enforcement evidence.
painPoints: Dashboards fail when they only show activity and not improved outcomes.
Leaders cannot defend renewals when economic return is disconnected from field action.

dependencies.name: Outcome Analytics and Benchmarking
dependencies.description: Computes outcome metrics, recurrence trends, benchmark views, and longitudinal value proof across sites and municipalities.
dependencies.how: Uses Outcome Analytics and Benchmarking to normalize incident, cleanup, and financial outcomes.

dependencies.name: Executive Impact Dashboard
dependencies.description: Presents outcome, financial, and operational value proof for elected leaders and municipal executives.
dependencies.how: Uses Executive Impact Dashboard to present the proof in a council-ready form.

steps.title: Communicate transparent action
keyFacts: Public legitimacy depends on visibility and proportionality, not only on results.
Signage and communication material are part of the operating model.
painPoints: Resident trust weakens when councils cannot explain why and how deployments exist.
Transparency work becomes inconsistent when communication is not built into the system.

dependencies.name: Resident Communication and Signage Kit
dependencies.description: Packages site signage, resident-facing communication, and public information material required for transparent municipal deployment.
dependencies.how: Uses Resident Communication and Signage Kit to support transparent deployment and public messaging.

capabilities.name: Operate GDPR-compliant Public-space Enforcement
capabilities.description: Help municipalities run the program with clear privacy boundaries, retention discipline, and full accountability over access and deployment decisions.
outcomes: Higher municipal trust in deployment expansion

flows.title: Operate GDPR-compliant Public-space Enforcement
flows.description: Move from compliant deployment setup to accountable live operation.

steps.title: Define compliant deployment
keyFacts: A compliant deployment starts with justified siting and explicit privacy boundaries.
The municipality remains the data controller and needs clear supporting artifacts.
painPoints: Deployments stall when councils cannot assemble the governance pack fast enough.
Privacy confidence breaks when field boundaries are informal or poorly documented.

dependencies.name: DPIA and GDPR Compliance Workspace
dependencies.description: Packages deployment justifications, legal clauses, signage, and DPIA material required for compliant municipal operation.
dependencies.how: Uses DPIA and GDPR Compliance Workspace to prepare the governance basis for deployment.

dependencies.name: Privacy Zoning and Masking
dependencies.description: Applies configured capture boundaries and masking rules so monitoring remains focused on the relevant public area.
dependencies.how: Uses Privacy Zoning and Masking to define the approved field of view.

steps.title: Govern access and retention
keyFacts: Access and retention must stay controlled continuously, not only at deployment time.
Evidence lifecycle needs to reflect ongoing procedural reality.
painPoints: Manual governance reviews do not scale across an installed base.
Compliance risk rises when evidence access and deletion are not traceable.

dependencies.name: Access Control and Audit Trail
dependencies.description: Controls who can access cases and evidence, and provides full traceability over key actions across the platform.
dependencies.how: Uses Access Control and Audit Trail to keep access and operator actions accountable.

dependencies.name: Retention and Deletion Scheduler
dependencies.description: Applies legal retention windows and deletion rules, including exceptions for ongoing administrative or judicial procedures.
dependencies.how: Uses Retention and Deletion Scheduler to keep evidence lifecycle compliant.

capabilities.name: Launch a Compliant Neighborhood Safety Pilot
capabilities.description: Help municipalities extend the same field and compliance foundations into selected safety incidents without creating a second operating model.

flows.title: Launch a Compliant Neighborhood Safety Pilot
flows.description: Reuse the existing platform foundation for an adjacent municipal safety workflow.

steps.title: Approve safety governance pack
keyFacts: Adjacent expansion only works when governance reuse is credible.
The municipality needs to understand what is shared and what changes.
painPoints: Expansion slows when teams must invent a new compliance model for each use case.
Confidence drops when pilot governance does not clearly inherit from the waste model.

dependencies.name: DPIA and GDPR Compliance Workspace
dependencies.description: Packages deployment justifications, legal clauses, signage, and DPIA material required for compliant municipal operation.
dependencies.how: Uses DPIA and GDPR Compliance Workspace to adapt the existing governance pack for the pilot.

steps.title: Run shared response workflow
keyFacts: The pilot should reuse the same case and sharing model wherever possible.
Operators need the adjacent workflow to feel familiar, not separate.
painPoints: A second workflow surface multiplies training and coordination cost.
Partner response breaks when evidence-sharing rules are inconsistent.

dependencies.name: Enforcement Case Workspace
dependencies.description: Organizes incidents, evidence, people, case state, and actions into one municipal case record.
dependencies.how: Uses Enforcement Case Workspace to run the pilot in the same municipal operating surface.

dependencies.name: Interagency Evidence Sharing
dependencies.description: Packages and transmits controlled case and evidence material to partner authorities or municipal services.
dependencies.how: Uses Interagency Evidence Sharing to coordinate the pilot with the right partners.
